# dispatch

> Beta control plane for agent execution nodes: deploy one service, scale sandboxed agents with metrics on a shared workspace. Execution is producer/consumer over per-deployment queues, so capacity scales by adding consumers (local goroutines, Kubernetes pods, or serverless containers running `dispatch work`). Access is defined in an NGAC policy machine (NIST-style users, attributes, associations, prohibitions; flat per-tool policies compile into the same graph) and enforced by the sandbox with default deny, and agents can spawn sub-agents through policy-gated self-reference.

Go module `github.com/urmzd/dispatch`; binary `dispatch` with `serve` (control plane API) and `work` (remote consumer) commands.

## Documentation

- [README](README.md): features, installation, quick start, Kubernetes scaling
- [Architecture overview](docs/architecture/overview.md): package DAG, queue flow, sandbox model, roadmap
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): AI-facing conventions, commands, extension guide
- [Contributing](CONTRIBUTING.md): development workflow and commit convention

## Examples

- [Basic](examples/basic/main.go): deploy, scale, submit, observe with a sandboxed tool
- [NGAC](examples/ngac/main.go): access as a policy graph with attribute grants, prohibitions, and spawn objects
- [Saige agents](examples/saige/main.go): AI agents as workloads, including agent-spawns-agent

## Optional

- [Kubernetes manifests](deploy/k8s/dispatch.yaml): server + scalable worker fleet, validated on minikube
- [Security policy](SECURITY.md): the sandbox is a security boundary
- [Skills](skills/): portable agent skills for working in this repo
